claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit with git ls-files for tracked/untracked files plus fallback walks, excluding artifact roots, but the dirty working tree indicates real stress-testing is needed.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git ls-files output with fallback walkFiles() and excludes allowed write roots like artifact directories
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation that combines git-backed detection with fallback coverage

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' covering cached, other (untracked), and non-excluded files
evidence.why: Git-backed file discovery covers tracked, cached, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks directories excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Fallback file walk ensures coverage for non-git projects or when git commands fail

evidence.summary: fileDigest() computes SHA256 hash of file contents for change detection
evidence.why: Cryptographic digests enable precise mutation detection before and after agent execution

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Change detection logic identifies all boundary violations during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots captured before and after agent execution with artifact root excluded
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement captures baseline state and detects any project mutations

evidence.summary: Boundary violations converted to blockers and added to readiness assessment
evidence.why: Violations block PRD readiness with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION error codes

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test verifies detection of untracked file mutations
evidence.why: Test confirms git ls-files coverage includes untracked files outside excluded directories

evidence.summary: Gitignored boundary test verifies detection of .gitignore-excluded mutations
evidence.why: Test confirms boundary detects mutations even when files are gitignored

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test verifies fallback walk detects modifications while excluding artifact root
evidence.why: Test confirms fallback coverage for non-git projects and proper artifact exclusion

evidence.summary: Multiple evidence dispatch artifacts exist from previous runs (dispatch-20260531233706363-3z1qa6 through dispatch-20260601003207087-u35u8d)
evidence.why: Artifact evidence shows dispatch system has been executed but dirty working tree indicates need for stress-testing

evidence.summary: Git status shows 60+ modified files including core source, tests, and documentation
evidence.why: Dirty working tree confirms the status quo claim that demand evidence dispatch must be stress-tested

risks: Boundary audit implementation has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in production
Edge cases may exist in non-git projects with deep directory structures or large file counts
missing: Evidence of real Claude -p agent stress testing with boundary violations attempted
Performance benchmarks for large repositories with thousands of files
Edge case coverage for symlink handling, permission errors, or concurrent modifications

result.notes: Core boundary audit implementation is complete with git ls-files coverage, fallback walks, artifact exclusion, and comprehensive tests. Dirty working tree confirms need for stress-testing but does not block readiness.
